text: Machine learning is a type of artificial intelligence where computers learn from data. In supervised learning, the computer is given training data that includes both input information and the correct answers. For example, you might show the computer many pictures of cats and dogs, each labeled correctly. Using this data, the computer builds a model—a kind of mathematical rule system. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen pictures. The accuracy of the model is measured by how often its predictions are correct. By improving the training data and fine-tuning the model, we can make predictions more reliable.
